literal: lord, ruler, the one who rules; from √īś 'to rule'
invariant_definition: The Lord — *brahman* under the aspect of cosmic ruler, sustainer, dispenser of *karma*-fruits, and object of *upāsana*. All Vedāntic schools accept the existence of *Īśvara* and identify him with one of the personal deities (Viṣṇu in the Vaiṣṇava traditions, Śiva in Śaiva-influenced contexts). They differ on whether *Īśvara* is the highest description of *brahman* (Viśiṣṭādvaita, Dvaita, Vaiṣṇava bhedābheda — yes, the personal Lord is the highest reality) or a *saguṇa* description sublatable in the realization of *nirguṇa brahman* (Advaita).

per_school.definition: *Īśvara* is *saguṇa brahman* — *brahman* associated with *māyā* (under its *sattva*-pradhāna *upādhi*). *Īśvara* is the cosmic creator, sustainer, dissolver, and dispenser of *karma*-fruits. Real for the *vyāvahārika* and devotional standpoints; sublated in the realization of *nirguṇa brahman*. *Īśvara* is the proper object of *upāsana* but not the proper object of liberating *jñāna*.

*Īśvara* IS *brahman* in his highest description: Viṣṇu-Nārāyaṇa, possessing infinite auspicious qualities, with *cit* and *acit* as his body, the soul of all souls. There is no higher *nirguṇa* description; *nirguṇa* texts deny only *heya-guṇas*.
*Īśvara* is the unique *svatantra* — Viṣṇu, the only independent reality, on whom all else depends. Identified strictly with Viṣṇu against the Śaiva and other identifications.

*Īśvara* is Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*; Viṣṇu in his various forms is *Īśvara* as *paramātman*; impersonal *brahman* is the *brahmajyoti* (Kṛṣṇa's bodily effulgence). A graded ontology: *brahman* < *paramātman* < *bhagavān* (Kṛṣṇa).

translator_note: 'God' is the conventional gloss; bears the same risks flagged for *brahman* (Christian-monotheist resonance). 'The Lord' is closer (it preserves the rulership-sense of √īś). Note: in Advaita, calling *brahman* 'God' (as if *Īśvara* exhausts the highest description) is precisely the misreading — *nirguṇa brahman* exceeds *Īśvara*.
